Name: DBSA Gauteng Road Infrastructure Revenue Bond – N1/N14 Corridor (ZAR 4.2 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by projected toll and availability payment revenues from the N1 North (Pretoria–Bela-Bela) and N14 (Johannesburg–Krugersdorp) upgrade project. SANRAL/NT availability payment: ZAR 820M/yr. DSCR: 1.55×. Bond term: 15 years. FSCA-approved. JSE-listed. NT guarantee ref NT-IRB-0045. Rating: Moody's Baa2 (sovereign ceiling).

Name: DBSA KZN Umgeni Water Treatment Revenue Bond (ZAR 2.8 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by bulk water tariff revenues from the Umgeni Water Board's Midmar Dam to Durban bulk supply system (600 ML/day capacity). Annual tariff revenue: ZAR 680M. DSCR: 1.48×. Term: 12 years. Co-financed with EIB EUR 150M. DWS water use authorisation. DBSA infrastructure bond ref DBSA/IRB/KZN/2022/001. NT guarantee letter.

Name: DBSA Limpopo Schools Infrastructure Revenue Bond (ZAR 1.8 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by DBE availability payments for 120 new schools in Limpopo under ASIDI (Accelerated Schools Infrastructure Delivery Initiative). Annual DBE payment: ZAR 420M over 20-year DBOM concession. DSCR: 1.65×. PPP registered under Public Finance Management Act s.76. DBSA/DBE infrastructure delivery agreement LIM-ASIDI-2023. NT approval ref NT-PPP-0234.

Name: DBSA Eastern Cape Health Facilities Revenue Bond (ZAR 1.5 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by DoH availability payments for 8 new district hospitals and 45 community health centres in the Eastern Cape under DBSA Health Infrastructure Programme. Annual DoH payment: ZAR 360M. DSCR: 1.52×. Infrastructure delivery period: 2022–2026. DBOM maintenance period: 20 years. DBSA/DoH delivery agreement EC-HEALTH-2022. NT approval.

Name: DBSA Northern Cape Solar Energy Revenue Bond – REIPPP Round 6 (ZAR 2.1 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by Eskom PPA revenues from 5 REIPPP Round 6 solar PV projects (combined 480 MW) in the Northern Cape (John Taolo Gaetsewe, Frances Baard districts). Annual PPA revenue: ZAR 520M at CPI-indexed tariff. 20-year term. DSCR: 1.6×. GCF co-financing: USD 40M. DBSA ref DBSA/IRB/REN/2024/001. NERSA generation licences issued.

Name: DBSA PRASA Rolling Stock & Station Upgrade Revenue Bond (ZAR 3.5 billion)
Description: Revenue bond backed by PRASA ridership and government operations grant revenues from the New Rolling Stock Programme Phase II (120 new Gibela trains). PRASA annual operating grant: ZAR 9.2B; allocated debt service: ZAR 720M/yr. DSCR: 1.58×. Term: 14 years. NT guarantee. Infrastructure delivery: PRASA/Gibela SA JV. DBSA ref DBSA/IRB/PRASA/2023/001.

Name: DBSA Infrastructure Bond 2021A (ZAR 5 billion) – JSE Listed
Description: 15-year vanilla fixed-rate bond issued by DBSA on the JSE Debt Listings under its ZAR 30B Domestic Medium-Term Note (DMTN) Programme. Coupon 9.25% p.a. Proceeds fund infrastructure loans across South Africa's municipalities, SOEs and private sector. JSE listing ref JSE/DBSA/2021/001. NT guarantee. S&P: AA– (za) (National Scale). Moody's: Baa2.

Name: DBSA Infrastructure Bond 2022A (ZAR 8 billion) – JSE Listed
Description: 12-year fixed-rate DMTN bond, Coupon 9.75% p.a. Largest single DBSA bond issuance. Proceeds fund: Metro bus rapid transit networks (BRT), Joburg water and sanitation upgrades, municipal solid waste facilities and urban public transport hubs. JSE listing ref JSE/DBSA/2022/003. NT backstop guarantee.

Name: DBSA Green Bond 2023A (ZAR 3.5 billion) – CBI Certified
Description: 10-year green bond certified by the Climate Bonds Initiative (CBI) – Water Infrastructure and Renewable Energy categories. Coupon 9.0% p.a. Proceeds exclusively fund: solar and wind projects, water treatment upgrades, green public transport and climate-resilient housing. JSE Sustainability Segment listed. NT approval. S&P Green Bond Evaluation: E1/100.

Name: DBSA Social Bond – Affordable Housing 2023B (ZAR 2.8 billion)
Description: 8-year social bond for affordable housing delivery under the Breaking New Ground (BNG) programme and FLISP scheme. Target: 18,000 housing units in 6 provinces. Coupon 9.5% p.a. JSE Sustainability Segment listed. Aligned with ICMA Social Bond Principles. DHS/NHFC co-financing. NT approval ref NT/SB/DBSA/2023/001. S&P Social Bond Evaluation: S1/95.

Name: DBSA Sustainable Infrastructure Bond 2024A (ZAR 6.2 billion) – JSE Listed
Description: 15-year sustainable infrastructure bond under DBSA's updated Sustainable Finance Framework (SFF 2024) aligned with UN SDGs. Coupon 10.25% p.a. JSE DMTN Programme. Proceeds target: energy transition (JET-IP alignment), water resilience, healthcare infrastructure and digital public infrastructure. NT guarantee. S&P: AA– (za). CBI second-party opinion by Sustainalytics.

Name: DBSA–AfDB Long-Term Infrastructure Credit Line (EUR 500 million)
Description: Long-term credit line from the African Development Bank to DBSA for on-lending to South African and SADC infrastructure projects. AfDB loan ref ADB/RSA/DBSA/2022/001. 25-year term, 5-year grace period, AfDB benchmark + 0.8% margin. On-lent to municipalities (40%), energy projects (35%) and transport (25%). NT sovereign guarantee.

Name: DBSA–World Bank IBRD On-Lending Credit Line (USD 500 million)
Description: IBRD Enclave Lending Facility channelled through NT to DBSA for municipal infrastructure on-lending. World Bank project P178234A. Term: 20 years, 5-year grace, IBRD fixed rate + NT margin. On-lent to 22 municipalities for water, sanitation, roads and electricity. NT guarantee. Annual disbursement tranches subject to DPME performance review.

Name: DBSA NT Government-Backstop Guarantee Instrument (ZAR 40 billion)
Description: GRZ/NT sovereign backstop guarantee covering DBSA's total senior unsecured debt portfolio (ZAR 40B as at December 2024). Guarantee issued under PFMA s.70 and DBSA Act No. 13 of 1983. Annual NT review. Enables DBSA's investment grade ratings (Baa2/BBB–/AA–(za)) on international and domestic capital markets. NT guarantee ref NT-BACKSTOP-DBSA-2024.

Name: DBSA–EIB Climate Finance Facility (EUR 300 million)
Description: European Investment Bank climate finance facility for DBSA on-lending to South African renewable energy, water resilience and low-carbon transport projects. EIB agreement EIB/RSA/DBSA/2024/001. 20-year term, 5-year grace, EIB Climate Action Lending Rate. First disbursement: EUR 100M to REIPPP Round 6 projects. DBSA sustainability committee oversight.

Name: DBSA N2 Rural PPP Token – Eastern Cape (ZAR 4.8 billion)
Description: PPP token for the N2 Rural Upgrade Project in the Eastern Cape: 180 km of N2 between Mthatha and Kokstad under a 25-year DBOT concession. Private concessionaires: WBHO–Raubex–Royal Bafokeng consortium. NT/SANRAL availability payment: ZAR 920M/yr. DBSA senior lending: ZAR 2.8B. NT PPP approval ref NT-PPP/EC/N2/2023/001. FSCA registered.

Name: DBSA eThekwini Water & Sanitation PPP Token (ZAR 3.2 billion)
Description: PPP token for the eThekwini Metropolitan Municipality 20-year water and sanitation DBOM concession: water treatment capacity upgrade to 840 ML/day, 120,000 new household connections in informal settlements. Private operator: Veolia SA–Mott MacDonald consortium. DWS Blue Drop and Green Drop certified programme. DBSA financing: ZAR 1.8B. NT Treasury approval.

Name: DBSA Gauteng Schools ESCO PPP Token (ZAR 1.8 billion)
Description: PPP token for the DBSA–Gauteng Department of Education Energy Services Company (ESCO) programme: solar PV rooftop, LED retrofitting and smart metering for 620 public schools in Gauteng. ESCO saves GDE ZAR 280M/yr in energy costs. DBSA green finance: ZAR 1.2B. GDE availability payment: ZAR 195M/yr. PFMA s.76 PPP registration. NT approval NT-PPP/GP/GDE/2024/003.

Name: DBSA REIPPP Round 6 Renewable Energy PPP Token (ZAR 6.5 billion)
Description: PPP token for DBSA's senior lending portfolio in 12 REIPPP Round 6 preferred bidder projects (solar 840 MW, wind 620 MW, storage 380 MWh). DBSA senior debt: ZAR 6.5B across 12 project finance vehicles. Eskom PPAs and GRZ partial risk guarantee (PRG). IFC B-Loan: USD 180M. Green Climate Fund co-financing: USD 85M. NT PPP approval ref REIPPP-R6-DBSA-2024.

Name: DBSA Concession Right – eThekwini Water Infrastructure 20-Year DBOM
Description: Tokenised 20-year Design-Build-Operate-Maintain (DBOM) concession right for eThekwini water infrastructure granted to DBSA-led Veolia SA consortium. Concession covers Durban Metropolitan Water Treatment Works (840 ML/day), pump stations (42), and reticulation mains (1,200 km). Annual concessionaire revenue: ZAR 1.4B. DWS concession registration WCC-ETH-2024.

Name: DBSA Pan-African Infrastructure Fund – SADC Concession Portfolio Right
Description: Tokenised concession management right for DBSA's Pan-African Infrastructure Development Fund (PAIDF) II, managing USD 650M in infrastructure concession equity across 14 SADC countries: roads, ports, energy and telecoms. DBSA fund manager, 25% carried interest. Fund term: 2018–2030. AUM: USD 650M. PAIDF II LP units held by African sovereign wealth funds and DFIs.

Name: DBSA N2 Eastern Cape – 25-Year DBOT Concession Right
Description: Tokenised 25-year Design-Build-Operate-Transfer (DBOT) concession right for the N2 Eastern Cape rural upgrade between Mthatha and Kokstad (180 km). Concession granted by SANRAL under the South African National Roads Agency Act. Includes operation and maintenance responsibility, weighbridge revenue and SANRAL availability payment. Annual concession revenue: ZAR 920M. SANRAL concession agreement N2/EC/DBOT/2023.

Name: DBSA Equity Token – Government of SA 100% Shareholder Holding
Description: Tokenised equity representing the South African Government's 100% ownership of DBSA through the Department of Public Enterprises (DPE). DBSA total assets: ZAR 148B (Q4 2024 audited). Net equity: ZAR 62B. DBSA development mandate under the DBSA Act. Annual shareholder compact reviewed by DPE and NT. DBSA balance sheet rated investment grade by S&P, Moody's and Fitch.

Name: DBSA Equity – REIPPP Renewable Energy Portfolio Stake (25 projects)
Description: Tokenised equity representing DBSA's strategic equity stakes (average 20%) in 25 REIPPP operational project companies across Rounds 1–5. Combined installed capacity: 2,800 MW (solar, wind, storage). Annual dividend income: ZAR 420M. Equity book value: ZAR 8.2B. Projects co-developed with IPPs: Scatec, BioTherm, Red Cap, Mulilo, FRV, ENGIE, and others.

Name: DBSA Equity – Pan-African Infrastructure Development Fund II (Fund Manager Stake)
Description: Tokenised equity representing DBSA's 20% stake in the PAIDF II General Partner and 5% LP co-investment. Total fund size: USD 650M. DBSA committed capital: USD 130M. Fund portfolio: 18 infrastructure assets across 14 SADC/AU countries. DBSA GP rights include carried interest (20% above 8% hurdle) and management fee (1.5% of AUM). Fund term: 2018–2030.
